summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a spring‑78 show that dazzles with a smoking Scarlet > Fire transition, soaring Ramble On Rose and crisp drums.
review: The Miller‑sourced SBD is praised as top‑shelf, with clear instrument separation despite occasional pitch‑correction quirks. Musically the concert is hailed as one of ’78’s best – high‑energy first set, a standout Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain sequence, and memorable Ramble On Rose, Big River and Music Never Stopped performances.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews overwhelmingly rate the show 4‑5 stars, highlighting both superb playing and excellent recording quality; only a few isolated criticisms keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, wide‑ranged SBD with Miller’s mastering; some users note channel swap, pitch‑correction and occasional fast playback, but overall the mix is clear, balanced and superior to other sources.

standout_songs: Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain
poor_songs: Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain (one reviewer’s personal dislike)
Werewolves of London (called painful by a reviewer)
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set rockets off with Promised Land and stays high‑energy, featuring a Stay‑in‑Alive disco tease; second set maintains momentum with the explosive Scarlet‑Fire transition, strong Terrapin/Black Peter, and a raucous Werewolves encore.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for his ‘Wolf’ guitar tone, soaring solos on Scarlet and Fire, and strong vocals (though one reviewer noted his singing was marginal).
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s Rhodes work is highlighted as simple yet effective; he adds the Stay‑in‑Alive tease and quotes during Big River.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drum segments repeatedly singled out as a highlight – howler‑monkey sounds, energetic fills, and an unforgettable Drums > Space section.
